appeal_letter:
To the Appeals Department,

I am appealing the denial of coverage for the MRI of the wrist performed on 06/02/2026 at an out-of-network radiology center.

The denial states that my plan requires prior authorization for all advanced imaging, and specifically requires out-of-network services to obtain a Network Exception authorization before the service is rendered. The denial indicates that no such authorization was requested or approved, and that because this was not a life-threatening emergency, I am responsible for the out-of-network charges. The single ground for the denial is therefore the absence of a pre-service Network Exception authorization for the out-of-network imaging.

Applied to the documented facts, this requirement supports coverage of the imaging, or at minimum warrants an individualized exception to the pre-service timing. After a fall while skateboarding, I was seen at an urgent care where X-rays were negative but the treating doctor suspected a scaphoid fracture, a condition that can cause bone death if missed. The doctor sent me immediately to the imaging center next door. The referral was urgent and physician-directed, and the imaging center, which was out of network, did not run a prior authorization because of the urgent nature of that referral. The purpose of the Network Exception requirement is to direct care to in-network providers when feasible; here, the documented urgency of a same-visit physician referral for a suspected scaphoid fracture made obtaining a pre-service authorization impracticable, which is the kind of circumstance an individualized exception is meant to address.

The denial did not state the basis on which urgency or medical necessity was evaluated, and it did not disclose my appeal rights or any applicable appeal deadline. I request that these be provided.

I request that the denial be overturned and that the MRI of the wrist performed on 06/02/2026 be covered, or in the alternative that an individualized Network Exception be granted for this service based on the documented urgent referral.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Urgent care visit records and the treating physician's note documenting the suspected scaphoid fracture and the same-visit referral for MRI
The negative wrist X-ray report from the urgent care visit
Written or documented referral from the urgent care physician directing the patient to the imaging center
The radiology report and MRI findings from the 06/02/2026 study
The plan's policy language defining the Network Exception requirement and any urgent-referral or retroactive-authorization exceptions
Documentation of whether any in-network imaging center was reasonably available at the time of the urgent referral
The itemized out-of-network charges being billed to the member
The plan's stated appeal rights and applicable appeal deadline
